author: codex
fact: Phase 1 asset CLI and HQR/fixture pipeline are live under port/src/assets/, with rebuildable outputs under work/port/phase1/.
rationale: The Phase 1 workspace is implemented and validated; current asset work should build on the existing CLI and fixture path instead of recreating it.

author: codex
fact: The canonical MBN scrape snapshot is docs/mbn_reference/runs/20260213T-raw, with tools/mbn_workbench.py as the workbench pattern.
rationale: The checked-in corpus README names that run as canonical and the repo still uses the workbench split between checked-in inputs and generated query state.

author: codex
fact: Phase0 still owns canonical inputs, source roots, and golden targets for the port plan.
rationale: Later implementation work depends on the locked phase0 baseline instead of rediscovering assets, source ownership, or fixture targets ad hoc.

author: codex
fact: inspect-scene decodes typed scene metadata and track programs while preserving raw life blobs.
rationale: The current scene boundary already materializes structural scene/track data but keeps life bytes canonical until the blocked life evidence changes.

author: codex
fact: Only LM_DEFAULT and LM_END_SWITCH are active unsupported real-asset life blockers, and audit-life-programs is the canonical blocker report.
rationale: The full-archive audit found no other unsupported life ids in the current asset tree, so future life work should stay tightly scoped to those two opcodes.

author: codex
fact: inspect-background is separate from inspect-scene and uses classic zero-based HQR access where LBA_BKG.HQR[0] matters.
rationale: The checked-in background decoder is loader-faithful only if it can reach classic slot 0 and keep the header-plus-indirection path distinct from scene inspection.

author: codex
fact: Windows remains the canonical host for zig build test/run through the repo-local SDL2 layout under vcpkg_installed/x64-windows.
rationale: The build graph and helper scripts are explicitly Windows-first, so runtime verification should not be presented as host-agnostic until the dependency strategy changes deliberately.

author: codex
fact: Linux Bash is suitable for docs and source analysis, not the supported end-to-end zig build/run host.
rationale: The checked-in runtime path still depends on Windows SDL2 wiring and PowerShell-oriented helper scripts, so Linux remains an analysis host only for now.

author: codex
fact: The v2 memory tree replaced the old handoff-plus-mixed-log model, and port/ remains the only canonical implementation workspace.
rationale: The repo now keeps always-loaded memory small, pushes current-state truth into subsystem packs, and keeps implementation work rooted in port/ rather than parallel placeholders.

author: codex
fact: ISSUES.md is the companion repo trap log linked through the architecture memory surface, not a separate memory system.
rationale: Recurring repo traps need a durable home outside chat, but they should stay tied to the architecture pack and AGENTS workflow instead of becoming another always-loaded memory layer.

author: codex
fact: The checked-in fragment-bearing interior evidence pair is SCENE.HQR[11] plus LBA_BKG.HQR[10], and viewer-local GRM projection must treat zone maxima as boundary-aligned endpoints to accept that pair.
rationale: Asset-backed regressions and runtime startup now prove a positive fragment case, and the viewer only accepts it with fragment-zone cell counts computed as ((max - min) / unit) + 1.

author: codex
fact: The viewer's landed brick probes are deterministic brick-index placeholders, not actual decoded BRK raster output.
rationale: This older fact was correct before the BRK preview slice landed, but the later parser, CLI, viewer, and regression updates replaced it with actual palette-backed BRK preview evidence.

author: codex
fact: The background decoder now decodes selected BRK payloads into palette-backed preview swatches, and the viewer overlays those actual previews beside the synthetic brick probes for composition and fragment surfaces.
rationale: The live parser decodes BRK payload bytes into preview rasters, inspect-background and inspect-room report stable preview-library summaries for background 10, and the viewer plus tests now confirm actual preview lookup on both the canonical room and the checked-in 11/10 fragment pair.

author: codex
fact: The viewer-local room path now renders composition tops, fragment cells, and fragment comparison cards from decoded BRK previews and fails fast if a referenced preview is missing.
rationale: The current viewer draw path now routes every nonzero top-surface brick reference through the decoded BRK preview library instead of synthetic brick-index overlays, while keeping the existing comparison and navigation surfaces viewer-local.

author: codex
fact: Viewer render regression coverage now exercises the live renderDebugView path for the checked-in 11/10 fragment pair, the 2/2 zero-fragment pair, and missing BRK preview failures via a test-only SDL canvas trace.
rationale: The viewer already owned the comparison panel and BRK-backed debug surfaces; this slice adds deterministic render-path assertions without introducing a new room-art layer or handoff abstraction.

author: codex
fact: port/src/runtime/world_query.zig now owns explicit hero-start mapping diagnostics over immutable RoomSnapshot data, including raw mapped-cell probes, occupied-coverage evidence, and nearest occupied/standable candidates.
rationale: The guarded 19/19 runtime slice now distinguishes exact validation failure from heuristic candidates and mapping-mismatch evidence without moving spawn policy into room_state.zig or session.zig.

author: codex
fact: On the guarded 19/19 baseline, zone_world_point ranks above scene_object_world_point for bounded extra-anchor review, but both remain rejected and hero_start_world_point stays the only admitted floor-truth mapping anchor.
rationale: The runtime-owned investigation in world_query uses immutable room snapshots only. 19/19 zone bounds keep floor-grid-aligned Y extents but still prove trigger volumes rather than source-backed floor points, while the positioned non-origin scene object sample is off the 256-unit floor grid.

author: codex
fact: runtime/world_query.zig now exposes diagnostic-only local neighbor topology probes for occupied cells, reporting cardinal neighbor occupancy, standability, and raw top-surface Y deltas without movement policy or session integration.
rationale: The new probe stays inside the immutable room-snapshot query layer and is verified on the guarded 19/19 baseline against existing cell, surface, and standability queries.

author: codex
fact: runtime/world_query.zig now summarizes observed local neighbor patterns across occupied cells, counting raw neighbor statuses, occupied-neighbor standability, and sorted top-surface Y-delta buckets on the guarded 19/19 baseline.
rationale: The summary is built directly from the diagnostic local-neighbor probe and stays descriptive rather than introducing movement-policy taxonomy.

author: codex
fact: The guarded 19/19 neighbor-pattern regression is now fixed: all 4828 occupied neighbors are standable, there are zero blocked or missing-top-surface neighbors, and the only observed top-surface delta bucket is 0.
rationale: These pinned facts tell the next topology slice what the supported baseline actually contains before it invents any relation taxonomy.

author: codex
fact: runtime/session.zig now constructs Session from explicit world-position input, while runtime/room_state.zig owns the checked-in RoomSnapshot-to-hero-start adaptation boundary.
rationale: This lands the first DECISION_PLAN extraction target by removing direct RoomSnapshot-based session construction and centralizing the remaining LBA2-shaped hero-start adaptation in the mixed room_state boundary.

author: codex
fact: Windows validation now has a fast tier: zig build test-fast plus scripts/verify-viewer.ps1 -Fast for iteration, while bare zig build test plus scripts/verify-viewer.ps1 remain the canonical full gate.
rationale: The build graph now splits fast and slow test roots, the verifier script chooses only between 	est-fast and 	est while keeping the same staged-binary assertions, and zig build stage-viewer stages lba2-tool, lba2, and SDL2.dll for both verifier modes.

author: codex
fact: Default canonical memory pickup now stays on the roadmap, current_focus, relevant subsystem packs, and the live port tree; sidequest/ and LM_TASKS/ stay excluded until promoted.
rationale: The current-state docs and context renderer now point future sessions at the checked-in roadmap, memory packs, and live code while filtering independent sidequest and LM task work out of default pickup.

author: codex
fact: The guarded 19/19 zero-fragment viewer status now derives its cardinal FOCUS cues from MoveTargetStatus-based move-option evaluation in runtime/world_query.zig instead of the earlier topology-only neighbor probe.
rationale: viewer_shell now threads one locomotion status model with admitted current-cell move options, and the render-path tests pin movement-semantic HUD copy rather than standability-only cues.

author: codex
fact: scripts/verify-viewer.ps1 now accepts a confirmed viewer startup followed by a clean shutdown under automation, instead of requiring the lba2 process to stay live for the whole poll window.
rationale: The staged Windows viewer can emit the expected startup, room-snapshot, and status diagnostics before a scripted shutdown; the verifier now treats that as a valid launch confirmation while preserving the same staged-binary and inspect-room checks.

author: codex
fact: runtime/locomotion.zig now owns guarded 19/19 step results, while runtime/world_query.zig owns exact containing-zone queries; the admitted 39/6 fixture path currently yields an exact empty containing-zone set.
rationale: This lands the first Phase 5 Branch B runtime movement seam with viewer code reduced to formatting and input routing only, while preserving the checked-in scene-zone bounds as exact trigger-volume evidence instead of inventing a current-zone heuristic.

author: codex
fact: app/viewer_shell.zig now formats runtime-owned containing-zone membership as HUD ZONES ... lines and structured stderr zones=... fields for seeded, accepted, and admitted-position rejected guarded 19/19 statuses.
rationale: runtime/locomotion.zig already carries exact zone membership for the guarded admitted-path statuses; this slice keeps formatting viewer-local, makes the empty-set result explicit as HUD ZONES NONE plus stderr zones=none, and pins both surfaces in viewer-shell/render tests.

author: codex
fact: app/viewer/render.zig now projects admitted-path current-cell highlights plus status-colored N/E/S/W target cues on the guarded 19/19 schematic from viewer-supplied locomotion display payload, without querying runtime directly.
rationale: This lands the next diagnostics-only guarded 19/19 movement cue beyond HUD/stderr text by keeping world_query and locomotion as the runtime evidence owners, viewer_shell as the packaging owner, and render as the display-only schematic owner.

author: codex
fact: viewer_shell now exposes guarded 19/19 raw-start BOUNDS.../occupied_bounds=... coverage and admitted-path SURF.../current_footing=..., all from runtime-owned locomotion data.
rationale: The guarded 19/19 viewer path now makes raw-start coverage extents and admitted-path footing explicit without adding a new runtime query seam or viewer-side recomputation.

author: codex
fact: Admitted target_rejected steps now carry target occupied coverage, and viewer_shell prints target_occupied_coverage/target_occupied_bounds/dx/dz while raw-start and origin_invalid keep their prior contracts.
rationale: This slice reuses the existing target MoveTargetEvaluation inside applyStep, keeps world_query as the coverage owner, keeps viewer_shell formatting-only, and adds structured diagnostics without widening HUD or render contracts.
